mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains stable or rises without corresponding SPX declines, or if 20-day returns in easing periods show no significant outperformance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 0.5% monthly triggers central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.5% but SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns through 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 and UNRATE move in opposite directions without corresponding SPX return divergence, or interaction effect size < 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In monetary tightening regimes, rising consumer spending (RSAFS) signals resilient demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns despite higher rates over 60 days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 2.0% but RSAFS fails to correlate with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over 60-day horizon in tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises but SPX shows negative 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or IC < 0.10 in rolling 1-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1200 thousand units signal strong demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 1200 but SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or IC < 0.08 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment (PAYEMS) paired with falling unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns through 60 days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS and UNRATE move in opposite directions without SPX return correlation (Rank IC < 0.10) over 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: During demand expansion phases, growing GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal economic strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 and HOUST both rise but SPX shows negative 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction effect size < 0.12 in out-of-sample tests.
